_meta.methodology: Germany earnings structure survey aligned with EU SES. Covers enterprises 10+ employees. Reports gross monthly/annual earnings by occupation (KldB 2010 codes), federal state, enterprise size. Published every 4 years.
_meta.coverage: Germany national. Berlin derived from Bundesland (federal state) breakdown for Berlin.
_meta.limitations: KldB occupation codes map to broad groups. Seniority inferred from earnings distribution. Data is 2022 — market has shifted somewhat since then for tech roles.
_meta.currency_note: All figures in EUR gross annual.

records._ref: Destatis VSE 2022, KldB 2010 Group 43 — Software developers, Germany national
records.notes: Anforderungsniveau 2 = qualified professional, early stage. Gross annual earnings for software developers and programmers.

records._ref: Destatis VSE 2022, KldB 4311 — Software developers, Germany national mid
records.notes: Anforderungsniveau 3 = specialist level. Mid-career software developers. Consistent with Eurostat SES 2022 Germany data.

records._ref: Destatis VSE 2022, KldB 4311 — Software developers, Germany national senior
records.notes: Anforderungsniveau 4 = expert level. Senior software developers and architects.

records._ref: Destatis VSE 2022, KldB 4311 — Software developers, Berlin federal state
records.notes: Berlin federal state data from VSE. Berlin is slightly below the German national average for tech roles (lower cost of living, startup-heavy market). Approximately 4-5% below national median for software developers.

records._ref: Destatis VSE 2022, KldB 7311 — IT-Berater und IT-Analytiker (IT consultants/analysts), Germany
records.notes: IT consultants and analysts. Maps to business-analyst and data-analyst roles. Also used for product-manager normalization in absence of a more specific code.
Commercial management and business operations — maps to marketing-manager, sales-manager, operations-manager. Wide range; manager-level roles are at upper end.
